The struggle for individualism and the need for connection can and should coexist as both are valid and worthy attributes of our existence. I call it a struggle for individualism because of the conformist nature of our society (there are many paths of least resistance that lead towards conformist thinking), and I call it a need for connection because while it may not be as important as water, we cannot live in great health without it for very long. Finding and nurturing my inner voice and whole self strengthens the collective because by doing so I am giving permission to myself to be with integrity and honesty. This requires the practice of compassion since that journey is full of shameless learning from mistakes. Since loving connection between individuals cannot take place without vulnerability --dishonesty does not hold space for love--the more I embrace my uniqueness, the more I can embrace vulnerability, and the more I can embrace connection. I think of community as analogous, or more concretely, as a mirror of my whole self. I am routinely surprised to find myself recognizing reflections of others' traits and behaviors in myself, regardless of my relation to them. Loving my whole self, including characteristics which I find unpleasurable or embarrasing, requires that I love my whole community. The inverse is also true, although I find it simpler (not easier) to start from within.
